mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, higher borrowing costs for corporations and higher discount rates for equity valuation lead to negative forward returns for the S&P 500 as financial conditions tighten.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the unemployment rate signals an improving labor market and robust economic momentum, supporting corporate earnings expectations and leading to positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 1-month change in UNRATE and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 5-year rolling window (note: signal is inverted UNRATE change).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth is strong but housing starts are decelerating, it signals a divergence in consumer spending. This indicates the economic expansion is narrowing and becoming fragile, leading to negative forward equity returns as the market anticipates a broader slowdown.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in RSAFS > 5% AND Year-over-year % change in HOUST < 0%
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in over 55% of triggered instances, or the signal has a positive information coefficient over the full backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production, measured by its 3-month rate of change, directly reflects growing economic output and corporate activity, leading to upward revisions in earnings forecasts and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment, an upside surprise in inflation signals that the central bank will maintain or intensify its restrictive policy stance. This increases the risk of a hard landing and negatively impacts equity valuations almost immediately.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4%, the correlation between CPI surprises and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds a high threshold, it signals an overheating labor market. This stokes inflation fears and expectations of a hawkish monetary policy response, leading to negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods following a >300k PAYEMS print, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is not statistically different from the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: The year-over-year growth rate of real GDP is a direct measure of the health of the aggregate economy. Positive growth supports corporate revenue and earnings growth, leading to positive equity market performance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 5-year correlation between year-over-year GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative for a sustained period (more than 4 quarters).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low unemployment and high inflation (a hawkish Phillips Curve signal) forces the central bank to prioritize price stability over growth. The resulting restrictive monetary policy or expectation thereof leads to a contraction in equity multiples and negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% AND Year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL > 3.0%
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits positive average 60-day returns during periods when both trigger conditions are met over a full business cycle.
